title: astragalus uliginosus
intro: Astragalus uliginosus, also known as marsh milkvetch, is a perennial plant species in the legume family that grows in wet environments. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The plant has been traditionally used to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions, such as fatigue, bronchitis, and arthritis, thereby providing several health benefits. Astragalus uliginosus can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is often combined with other herbs in herbal preparations to enhance its therapeutic effects.
